title: Silky Japanese Salmon Sushi
description: A festive, umami-packed sushi that balances the richness of salmon with a silky texture achieved through precise sous‑vide cooking and seasoned vinegar rice.

ingredients.name: Sushi‑grade salmon fillet
ingredients.notes: skin removed, cut into 1.5 cm thick slices

ingredients.name: Sushi rice
ingredients.notes: short‑grain, rinsed until water runs clear

instructions: 2. Season salmon slices lightly with salt and place each in a separate vacuum‑sealed bag.
3. Cook the salmon sous‑vide for 30 minutes, then remove and pat dry.
4. In a saucepan, heat 200 ml of water with rice vinegar, sugar, and salt until sugar dissolves.
5. Rinse sushi rice until the water runs clear, then drain and transfer to the seasoned vinegar mixture; fold gently until the grains are glossy and coated.
6. Spread a thin layer of rice onto a bamboo mat, then place a nori sheet on top, shiny side down.
7. Arrange a salmon slice on the nori, followed by a few cucumber and avocado strips.
8. Carefully roll the mat to form a tight cylinder, sealing the edge with a little water.
9. Sear the rolls briefly in a hot, lightly oiled skillet or grill, searing only the outside for 10–15 seconds to enhance texture and flavor.
10. Slice the rolls into bite‑size pieces, serve with soy sauce, wasabi, and pickled ginger.
11. Enjoy the silky, umami‑rich salmon sushi as a festive treat.
